title: Bathroom & Kitchen fan-control PREVIEW bundle variants
description: Source-of-truth promotion of the Bathroom and Kitchen PoE room-bundle fan-control variants from planning-only (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-VARIANTS-001) to an explicit PREVIEW bundle plan (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-VARIANTS-002). The plan is that WebFlash easy mode becomes a bundle picker in which these fan-control variants MAY appear as Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated PREVIEW bundle products (with warnings); the stable / full release of every fan-control variant stays gated behind hardware / bench-evidence / compliance gates. This file remains documentation / source-of-truth metadata only: it adds no product YAML, no firmware config string, no artifact_name, no config/webflash-builds.json row, flips no webflash_build_matrix, publishes no firmware, and does NOT touch the WebFlash repo. Per-variant fields record the real firmware-config status verified against config/preview-release-targets.json, config/preview-fan-triac-build-rows.json, config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json, config/product-catalog.json, and config/webflash-builds.json. Missing full-bundle firmware configs are marked preview-planned-missing-config and are NOT silently exposed; a fan-only config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted for a room-bundle variant because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules. Relay / PWM / 0-10V (DAC) / TRIAC control are NOT interchangeable at runtime. Only Bathroom and Kitchen have fan variants; Corridor / Landing, Living Room, and Bedroom remain sensing / status bundles. Bathroom TRIAC is an ADVANCED / manual-warning preview only and is currently build-blocked (HW-005); Kitchen has NO TRIAC variant. See docs/sense360-room-bundles.md.

warning_copy.preview: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
warning_copy.advanced-preview: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.
warning_copy.preview-planned-missing-config: PLANNED PREVIEW — NO FIRMWARE BUILT YET. The full room-bundle firmware config for this fan-control variant has not been built or published, so it is NOT installable, NOT released, NOT buyable, and NOT exposed in WebFlash. A fan-only firmware config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules. This variant becomes an Advanced-install-only preview only after its full-composition firmware config is built and imported; stable / full release additionally requires hardware, bench evidence, and compliance sign-off.
warning_copy.preview-compile-validated: PREVIEW (COMPILE-VALIDATED) — the full room-bundle firmware config EXISTS (product YAML authored) and its hosted ESPHome compile has PASSED (Compile-only Firmware Validation run 26913592989; full ESPHome compile success), but NO preview .bin is published. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, NOT a customer default, and NOT buyable. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. It is NOT exposed in WebFlash. A fan-only firmware config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules.

It is therefore WebFlash-easy-mode preview-eligible as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated preview bundle. webflash_exposed stays false because no fan row is added to config/webflash-builds.json; the actual WebFlash one-click exposure is the separately queued downstream WebFlash-repo follow-up (WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001). Not stable, not recommended, not a default, not buyable.

This is the documented address-overridden room-bundle preview exception; see fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.

revision_note: ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 built the missing full-composition product YAMLs for the five buildable fan-control variants (Bathroom PWM/DAC, Kitchen Relay/DAC/PWM): each has a real products/ shim + products/bundles/ composition + a config/product-catalog.json hardware-pending row + a config/compile-only-targets.json target. ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-COMPILE-RESULTS-001 then recorded the hosted full ESPHome compile result for those five configs and promoted them from buildable-preview-compile-pending to buildable-preview-compile-validated: the Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (run 26913592989, ref main, compile_mode=full, 2026-06-04, ESPHome 2026.4.5) passed Metadata Validation and the Full ESPHome Compile, so each variant's firmware_config_evidence.compile_validation_status is now validated-full-compile with a compile_evidence block. The two FanDAC configs compiled with the REQUIRED 0x5A IC2 address override (see fan_dac_i2c_address_policy); the physical DIP-switch -> address mapping stays bench-pending under FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001. This is firmware-build proof only: TRIAC stays build-blocked (HW-005); Bathroom Relay is unchanged. No firmware published, no .bin, no release/tag, no WebFlash repo change, no config/webflash-builds.json row, nothing stable / recommended / default / buyable, no hardware / bench / compliance / safety proof.

compile_results.fandac_address_note: Bathroom DAC and Kitchen DAC compiled with the FanDAC IC2 address override fan_dac_2_i2c_address: "0x5A". This is a compile-time configuration only; the physical IC2 DIP-switch (SW2) -> I2C address mapping is NOT bench verified and remains pending under FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001. GP8403 0x59 must not be used with VentIQ/AirIQ (SGP41 @ 0x59 collision).

fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.rationale: The S360-312 FanDAC board carries two GP8403 DACs (package defaults IC1 @ 0x58, IC2 @ 0x59). Both Sense360 air-quality modules — VentIQ (S360-211) and AirIQ (S360-210) — carry an SGP41 VOC/NOx sensor at I2C 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus. So the FanDAC default IC2 @ 0x59 collides with the air-quality SGP41 in any VentIQ/AirIQ + FanDAC bundle.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.precise_rule: AirIQ/VentIQ conflict with FanDAC ONLY when the FanDAC GP8403 uses 0x59. With GP8403 IC2 relocated to a non-0x59 address (0x5A), VentIQ/AirIQ + FanDAC can coexist on the shared bus. GP8403 supports the address span 0x58-0x5F via its A0/A1/A2 hardware address pins.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.firmware_override: fan_dac_2_i2c_address: "0x5A" (set in the room-bundle product YAML; overrides the package default 0x59)
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.required_hardware_switch: The installer MUST set the S360-312 IC2 DIP switch (SW2) so IC2 reports 0x5A (A1=1; A0=A2=0), NOT the 0x59 default. IC1 (SW1) stays at 0x58.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.webflash_one_click_posture: The fandac_conflicts_with_airiq mutex in config/webflash-compatibility.json STAYS in force for the WebFlash one-click surface, which cannot set DIP switches; AirIQ + FanDAC stays out of the WebFlash firmware-combination grammar. These room-bundle DAC configs are advanced / manual previews behind the documented switch requirement, NOT one-click WebFlash builds. The conflict is NOT relaxed silently — it is refined to a documented hardware-address requirement.
